pattern_name: ROAS erosion in recent window vs. trailing 90d
symptom: Account-level ROAS in the last 30 days is materially below the trailing 90-day ROAS, with cost/conv rising in parallel.
evidence_cue: Google Ads > Overview or Campaigns view with date comparison: Last 30d vs Last 90d. Look for ROAS delta and cost/conv delta on the same line.
root_cause: Could be seasonal demand shift, intensified auction competition, attribution/conversion-window changes, or a structural mix shift in campaign types that lowered weighted-average ROAS.
fix: Run a year-over-year same-period comparison to isolate seasonal vs. structural causes. Verify conversion window and attribution settings haven't changed. Then drill into campaign-type mix to find the source of the dilution.
benchmark: Flag when 30d ROAS is >=8% below 90d ROAS AND cost/conv is up >=5% in the same window.

pattern_name: Budget shifted from Search to Shopping degrades blended ROAS
symptom: Search share of spend has dropped sharply YoY while Shopping share has grown; account ROAS is down even though Shopping ROAS looks acceptable in isolation.
evidence_cue: Campaign-type breakdown (Search vs Shopping vs PMax) showing share-of-spend YoY and per-type ROAS. Look for high-ROAS Search being cut and lower-ROAS Shopping scaled.
root_cause: Shopping wins cheap clicks but at lower conversion rate and lower ROAS than high-intent Search. Cutting Search loses the highest-margin traffic and replaces it with comparison-shopper clicks.
fix: Rebalance budget back toward Search for high-intent and branded/niche queries. Keep Shopping as a complementary layer rather than the primary driver. Re-baseline budgets to last year's mix where Search dominated.
benchmark: Concern when Shopping ROAS is <60% of Search ROAS while consuming >50% of spend.

pattern_name: Shopping mix-shift drags overall CR down
symptom: Overall account CR has dropped (e.g., 5.19% to 4.32%) coinciding with Shopping becoming the dominant campaign type.
evidence_cue: CR by campaign type, weighted by spend. Compare YoY blended CR to the per-type CRs.
root_cause: Shopping traffic is browse/compare intent; Search traffic is in-market with specific query intent and converts at a higher rate. As Shopping share rises, blended CR mathematically falls even if each campaign holds steady.
fix: Restore Search share or improve Shopping CR via merchant feed quality, promo extensions, price competitiveness audit, and tighter product segmentation. Don't read blended CR without mix-adjustment.
benchmark: Investigate when blended CR drops >=0.5pp YoY while Shopping share of spend rises >=15pp.

pattern_name: PMax budget cut starves automated channels
symptom: PMax spend and revenue have collapsed YoY (e.g., £16,420 → £2,569, ~85% cut). ROAS may have ticked up but absolute revenue contribution is gone.
evidence_cue: PMax campaign year-over-year cost and conversion value; check whether PMax campaigns are paused or running at floor budgets.
root_cause: PMax is being judged on ROAS in isolation rather than as a discovery layer across YouTube, Discover, Gmail, and Display. Starving it removes incremental top-of-funnel reach.
fix: Re-fund PMax with at least one well-fed campaign per market (UK/US) with strong audience signals (customer list, abandoned cart). Use it for catalog discovery and remarketing breadth, not as a one-to-one ROAS competitor to Search.
benchmark: Flag if PMax share of spend is <10% of paid budget in a catalog-driven account.

pattern_name: Shopping cannibalises Search and pits brand against marketplaces
symptom: Shopping is capturing queries that Search used to convert; Auction Insights for Shopping shows large competitors like Amazon, Shein, etc.
evidence_cue: Auction Insights report on Shopping campaigns; overlap between Shopping query themes and disabled/underfunded Search ad groups.
root_cause: Shopping is a price-comparison surface. When Search is cut, Shopping inherits queries but loses to marketplaces on price visibility, raising CPC and lowering ROAS simultaneously.
fix: Run Search and Shopping in parallel so high-intent queries are won by Search ad copy and Shopping serves browse/discovery. Use brand-protection Search to defend any branded queries Shopping is exposing.
benchmark: Investigate when Shopping CPC YoY is up >=30% and Search impression share has dropped >=20pp.

pattern_name: Shopping CPC inflation outpacing revenue growth
symptom: Average Shopping CPC has risen materially YoY (e.g., £0.25 → £0.35, +40%) without a matching lift in conversion value.
evidence_cue: Shopping campaigns: average CPC YoY, plotted against conversion value YoY and ROAS.
root_cause: More aggressive auction competition (often from marketplaces), feed-quality drift, or bid strategies chasing share. Often combined with cannibalisation pattern.
fix: Tighten product segmentation, separate best-sellers under a Target ROAS strategy, demote or exclude poor performers, and reduce reliance on broad catalog Shopping in favour of curated PMax with audience signals.
benchmark: Concern when Shopping CPC YoY +>=25% and Shopping ROAS YoY -<=15%.

pattern_name: Shopping campaigns not segmented by performance tier
symptom: All products run in a single Shopping campaign with one bid strategy, regardless of whether they are best-sellers or long-tail.
evidence_cue: Shopping campaign list shows a catch-all campaign with no priority tiers; product groups show wide variance in ROAS within one campaign.
root_cause: Bidding is averaged across performance tiers, so winners are under-bid and losers are over-bid. Budget pacing favours volume over margin.
fix: Split Shopping into at least two campaigns: Best Sellers (Target ROAS) and Everything Else (Maximize Conversion Value with a ceiling, or manual). Use priority settings so the right campaign captures the right query.
benchmark: Apply when a single Shopping campaign contains >=200 SKUs spanning a >=3x ROAS range across product groups.

pattern_name: Missing hour-of-day bid adjustments on high-CPA windows
symptom: Cost/conv is significantly higher in specific hours (e.g., 12AM–4AM) but no ad schedule bid adjustment is applied.
evidence_cue: Reports > Time > Hour pivot for each campaign; cost/conv variance across hours; the Ad schedule tab shows no modifiers.
root_cause: Smart bidding handles some of this, but on portfolio Target ROAS or Manual CPC campaigns, dead hours waste budget at full bid.
fix: Apply negative bid adjustments (start at -20%) on the worst CPA hours; consider day-parting for the lowest-converting windows. For multi-region accounts (UK/US), build the schedule per timezone, not on blended data.
benchmark: Adjust hours where cost/conv is >=50% above account average over 90 days with >=100 clicks.

pattern_name: Brand Protection campaign with extremely high ROAS but rising spend
symptom: A brand-defense campaign shows ROAS >30 but absolute spend is climbing, indicating active competitor bidding on the brand term.
evidence_cue: Brand campaign with ROAS >>10; Auction Insights showing competitors on branded terms.
root_cause: Competitors bidding on the brand name are forcing higher CPCs to maintain top-of-page on brand SERPs. Healthy ROAS hides rising defensive cost.
fix: Confirm competitor presence in Auction Insights; if absent, test lowering brand bids to find the floor. If present, hold the line but monitor incrementality—some brand spend is replacing organic clicks.
benchmark: Investigate when brand campaign spend is up >=30% YoY despite stable branded search volume.

pattern_name: PMax paused while Shopping is live—losing automated reach
symptom: Change history shows PMax campaigns paused (or running intermittently) while Shopping is active.
evidence_cue: Change history; campaign status timeline; YouTube/Discover placement reports near empty.
root_cause: PMax is being treated as an either/or with Shopping. Pausing it removes incremental Display, YouTube, Discovery, and Gmail reach that Shopping cannot serve.
fix: Run PMax alongside Shopping with clear product exclusions to prevent SKU overlap. Treat PMax as the automated multi-surface layer; Shopping as the precision feed layer.
benchmark: Flag any account where catalog-driven PMax is paused for >=14 consecutive days.

pattern_name: Tablet traffic over-priced with no device bid adjustment
symptom: Tablet shows higher CPA and lower ROAS than desktop and mobile, but no device bid adjustment is in place.
evidence_cue: Devices report at account and campaign level; cost/conv and ROAS by device.
root_cause: Default device targeting equalises bids; tablets typically convert worse on e-commerce checkouts but pull paid impressions at full bid.
fix: Apply a -20% bid adjustment on tablet at the campaign level (or account-wide if pattern is consistent). Re-evaluate every 60 days.
benchmark: Apply when tablet CPA is >=30% above mobile/desktop blended CPA across 90 days, with >=200 clicks.

pattern_name: Desktop shows higher AOV but no positive bid adjustment
symptom: PC/desktop AOV is meaningfully higher than mobile, yet bidding is flat across devices.
evidence_cue: Devices report sorted by AOV (Conv. value / conversions).
root_cause: Standard bidding ignores AOV; you're under-investing on the device that delivers larger basket sizes.
fix: Test a +10% desktop bid adjustment on campaigns where the gap is consistent; review at 30/60 days.
benchmark: Test when desktop AOV is >=20% above mobile AOV across 90 days.

pattern_name: Cities with meaningful spend and zero orders over 24 months
symptom: Geographic report shows cities accumulating spend over two years with zero conversions.
evidence_cue: Locations report at city level over 24 months, sorted by cost descending, filtered to conversions = 0.
root_cause: Auto-targeting and Smart Bidding still bid into these areas because they look similar to converting cities at the audience-signal level. No human-applied negative location or bid adjustment.
fix: For cities with substantial spend, apply a -50% bid adjustment first, then exclude entirely if behaviour persists for another 30–60 days.
benchmark: Act when city spend >=2x average CPA over 24 months with 0 conversions.

pattern_name: Cities with consistently high CPA / low ROAS need bid suppression
symptom: A cohort of cities converts but at a CPA well above target or ROAS well below target across multi-year data.
evidence_cue: Locations report sorted by cost/conv descending and by ROAS ascending; look for repeat offenders across 24 months.
root_cause: Geographic price sensitivity, demographic mismatch, or last-mile/shipping friction unique to those locations.
fix: Apply -20% bid adjustment to the cohort. Revisit after 60 days; escalate to -50% or exclude if no improvement.
benchmark: Apply when city CPA is >=50% above target AND ROAS is <60% of target across 24 months.

pattern_name: High-spend search terms with zero conversions
symptom: Search Terms report contains queries with material spend (e.g., several hundred GBP) and zero conversions over 90 days.
evidence_cue: Search Terms report > 90 days > sort by Cost desc, filter Conversions = 0.
root_cause: Match types too broad (especially broad match), no campaign- or account-level negative keyword list scaling, or generic queries pulled in by Smart Bidding learning.
fix: Add as negative keywords. Build a tiered negative keyword list (account-level for absolute no-go terms; campaign-level for context-specific). Revisit weekly while ramping.
benchmark: Negate any search term with >=2x target CPA spend and 0 conversions across 90 days.

pattern_name: Shopping SKUs with material spend and zero conversions
symptom: Products with >£12 spend and zero conversions in 90 days remain in the active Shopping campaign.
evidence_cue: Products report (Shopping) filtered by spend threshold and Conversions = 0; aggregate wasted spend (e.g., £414 in audit).
root_cause: No SKU-level pruning; all products inherit campaign bids regardless of historical performance.
fix: Exclude these SKUs from the main Shopping campaign; spin up a standalone PMax with high Target ROAS and strong audience signals (customer list, abandoned cart) to test recovery before deprecating.
benchmark: Threshold: spend >=£10 (or 1.5x avg product CPC × 30 clicks) and 0 conversions in 90 days.

pattern_name: Catch-all Shopping campaign with low ROAS needs restructure or migration
symptom: A catch-all Shopping campaign (e.g., 'USAGS | Catch All | L') has consistently low ROAS and large wasted-spend pockets.
evidence_cue: Campaign ROAS below account target; high overlap of low-performing SKUs and irrelevant search terms; sparse negative keyword list.
root_cause: Catch-all campaigns inherit feed-wide product coverage with no segmentation or curation. Negative lists rarely scale at the same pace as the feed.
fix: Two options: (a) migrate winners to a curated PMax with audience signals and exclude losers; (b) keep Shopping but scale negative keyword list, add product-group bid tiers, and prune the tail.
benchmark: Restructure when a catch-all campaign ROAS is <60% of account target for 60+ days.

pattern_name: Negative keyword list not scaled with campaign growth
symptom: Negative keyword count is small relative to the volume of irrelevant search terms surfacing weekly.
evidence_cue: Shared library > Negative keyword lists; count of negatives vs. count of new wasteful search terms in the Search Terms report.
root_cause: Negative list maintenance not on a recurring cadence; broad match keywords expanding faster than negatives can keep up.
fix: Build a weekly Search Terms review cadence. Maintain a master negative list per market and theme (junk queries, competitor names, irrelevant intents).
benchmark: If <=10 new negatives added per month while >=50 new wasteful queries appear weekly, the list is under-maintained.

pattern_name: Time-of-day performance not analysed per campaign / per timezone
symptom: Account uses default round-the-clock bidding with no campaign-specific schedule adjustments, and account spans multiple timezones (UK + US).
evidence_cue: Ad schedule tab empty; Hours/Days report shows clear CPA variance not reflected in bid modifiers.
root_cause: Blended-data analysis hides per-timezone patterns. Without splitting by market and re-pivoting to local time, off-hours waste persists.
fix: Pull Hour-of-Day report per market, convert to local time, identify the bottom-quartile hours for cost/conv, and apply schedule adjustments at the campaign level.
benchmark: Run when account spans >=2 timezones and ad schedule modifiers are empty.

pattern_name: Revert to last year's higher-intent mix when YoY ROAS drops
symptom: Current-year structure (Shopping-heavy) underperforms last year's structure (Search/PMax-heavy) on ROAS and revenue.
evidence_cue: YoY campaign-type comparison with cost, conv value, ROAS; identify the higher-performing build.
root_cause: Strategy drift without rigorous A/B; new structure was scaled before being validated against prior baseline.
fix: Reactivate the higher-intent Search campaigns from the prior year's build, rebuild PMax with audience signals, and treat Shopping as a complementary layer.
benchmark: Reconsider when YoY ROAS down >=15% AND prior-year campaign mix delivered higher absolute revenue.

pattern_name: Insufficient remarketing presence in paid mix
symptom: No dedicated remarketing campaigns; PMax not configured with retargeting audience signals; brand sees high reliance on email (Klaviyo) as the last touch.
evidence_cue: Audience manager: remarketing lists exist but are not driving campaign signals; no dedicated Display/Discovery remarketing; Analytics shows multi-touch paths funneling to Klaviyo.
root_cause: Paid is acting as pure acquisition; remarketing has been outsourced entirely to email, leaving conversion paths thin between first touch and email capture.
fix: Launch a remarketing PMax (or Display + Discovery campaigns) with customer-list and cart-abandoner signals. Goal: reduce reliance on email as the sole closer and lift first-touch paid contribution.
benchmark: Investigate when paid remarketing is <5% of paid spend and email is >50% of last-touch revenue.

pattern_name: PMax running without customer-list or abandoned-cart audience signals
symptom: PMax campaign asset groups have no audience signal, or signals are demo-only.
evidence_cue: PMax campaign > Asset groups > Audience signal panel empty or generic.
root_cause: Audience signals are optional in PMax setup, but without them the system relies entirely on Google's modelled signals and ramps slower with worse early ROAS.
fix: Add Customer List (purchasers, high-LTV cohort) and engagement audiences (cart abandoners, product viewers) as signals. Refresh quarterly.
benchmark: Apply to any catalog PMax without at least one first-party audience signal.

pattern_name: Last-click attribution undervalues paid search vs. data-driven / paid-channels view
symptom: GA4 'Google paid channels' attribution credits paid search with ~28% of conversions and 25% of revenue, materially higher than last-click view.
evidence_cue: GA4 > Advertising > Attribution > Model comparison: last-click vs paid-channels vs data-driven. Compare paid search conversions across models.
root_cause: Paid search often initiates the conversion path; final touch is email (Klaviyo). Last-click hides paid's true contribution and leads to budget under-investment.
fix: Use paid-channels or data-driven attribution for budget decisions on Google Ads. Consider increasing paid spend on the under-credited campaigns (Search + PMax) where multi-touch credit shows incremental contribution.
benchmark: Re-evaluate when paid search share differs by >=10pp between last-click and data-driven attribution.

pattern_name: Revive previously high-performing Search campaigns that were turned off
symptom: Last year's Search campaigns delivered ROAS ~8.86 but have been paused or budget-starved this year.
evidence_cue: Change history showing pause events; YoY Search campaign performance.
root_cause: Campaigns paused during the strategy shift without an A/B validation that the replacement was superior.
fix: Reactivate the top historical Search campaigns at modest budgets, monitor for 30 days, and scale the winners. Use prior keyword lists and ad copy as the starting point.
benchmark: Revive any paused campaign that previously ran >=60 days at ROAS >=1.5x current account ROAS.

pattern_name: PMax without account-level negative keywords cannibalises brand and generic queries
symptom: PMax appears in brand-name search queries and generic comparison queries, eroding margin and competing with brand Search.
evidence_cue: PMax insights > Search categories; Auction Insights overlap with brand campaign; search terms surfacing in PMax insights.
root_cause: Default PMax has no negative keyword support beyond account-level brand exclusions (and lists added via support). Without exclusions, PMax claims branded and generic queries the brand was getting cheaply elsewhere.
fix: Request brand exclusions via Google support or via the brand-exclusion UI; add account-level negative keyword list once available in your account; run a dedicated Brand Protection Search campaign in parallel.
benchmark: Apply to any PMax that does not have brand exclusions enabled when a Brand Search campaign is also running.

pattern_name: GA4 purchase events missing cart data / item_id mismatch with Merchant Center
symptom: Google Ads conversion diagnostics flags problems with cart data quality for the GA4 purchase conversion (e.g., ~17% of purchase events missing cart data; 100% of sold item IDs unmatched in Merchant Center).
evidence_cue: Google Ads > Conversions > Diagnostics; check 'items', 'item_id', 'value', and 'currency' on the purchase event in GA4 DebugView and GTM Preview.
root_cause: GA4 purchase event implementation isn't sending the full items array, or product IDs in the data layer don't match Merchant Center product IDs. Often a GTM mapping issue.
fix: Audit the GA4 purchase event implementation; verify items array contains item_id, value, currency. Ensure item_id values exactly match Merchant Center product IDs. Test purchase end-to-end and recheck diagnostics.
impact_area: Conversion data quality, PMax/Shopping bidding accuracy
benchmark: Investigate when >5% of purchase events are missing cart data or any item_id mismatch rate is >0%.

pattern_name: Transactions tracked with static $1 value instead of dynamic revenue
symptom: Google Ads conversions show a flat $1 (or any fixed) value per purchase regardless of actual order amount.
evidence_cue: Conversions table: 'Conv. value' / Conversions = 1.00 across all rows. GTM purchase tag uses a hard-coded value parameter.
root_cause: GTM or gtag implementation passes a constant in the 'value' field rather than reading the dynamic order total from the data layer or backend.
fix: Update the purchase tag to pull the dynamic transaction value from the data layer (e.g., {{DLV - ecommerce.purchase.value}}). Validate via DebugView and a real test transaction. Rebuild ROAS-based strategies once the data is clean.
benchmark: Apply when conv-value-per-conversion has zero variance across orders with known different totals.

pattern_name: Cart conversion value inflated above purchase value
symptom: The 'Add to cart' conversion pulls the full order total per fire, while the 'Purchase' conversion is fixed or undervalued — so cart appears more valuable than purchase to bidding.
evidence_cue: Compare Conv. value / Conv. for Add-to-cart vs Purchase; cart > purchase indicates a value misconfiguration.
root_cause: Different conversion actions inherit different value sources (one dynamic, one static); upper-funnel event gets full transaction value while purchase doesn't.
fix: Realign value rules: purchase should carry the highest, dynamic, transaction value. Cart should be either zero-value (intent only) or a fixed sub-value below purchase. Don't optimise on both at full transaction value.
benchmark: Flag when average value-per-cart >= average value-per-purchase.

pattern_name: Engagement events (clicks, scrolls, pageviews) configured as primary conversions
symptom: Primary conversions list includes click, scroll, or pageview events alongside (or instead of) form submits and purchases.
evidence_cue: Tools > Conversions; check 'Account default' settings and which actions are marked Primary.
root_cause: Imported all GA4 events without filtering. Often happens when a non-specialist links GA4 conversions to Ads.
fix: Demote engagement events to Secondary (or Observation). Keep only actions tied to revenue or qualified leads as Primary. Re-train smart bidding on the cleaned conversion set.
benchmark: Concern when any non-revenue, non-lead event is marked Primary and has fired in the last 30 days.

pattern_name: All campaigns use account-level conversion settings — no per-campaign scoping
symptom: Every campaign optimises against the same primary conversion bundle, even when their goals differ (lead-gen vs. store visit vs. purchase).
evidence_cue: Campaign settings > Conversions > 'Account-default actions' selected; no campaign overrides.
root_cause: Account-level default applies the whole primary conversion list to every campaign. PMax/Search learn against mismatched objectives.
fix: Set campaign-level conversion goals: e-com campaigns optimise for Purchase only; lead campaigns for Form Submit / Call; store-visit campaign for Store Visits. Override account default per campaign.
benchmark: Apply to any account that runs >=2 distinct business objectives but has 0 campaign-level conversion overrides.

pattern_name: Conversions stuck in 'Need attention' status
symptom: Tools > Conversions shows multiple actions with status 'Needs attention' or 'No recent conversions' for >=20 days.
evidence_cue: Conversions table status column.
root_cause: Tag firing broken, site changes deprecated triggers, or the conversion was set up but never fired in production.
fix: Audit each affected tag in GTM Preview and Tag Assistant. Repair or remove. Don't leave broken Primary conversions live — they confuse Smart Bidding.
benchmark: Flag any Primary conversion with status 'Needs attention' for >=14 days.

pattern_name: Google Ads not linked to Google Analytics 4
symptom: Audience import, GA4 conversion import, remarketing list creation, and bid-strategy enrichment are all unavailable because the GA4–Ads link is missing.
evidence_cue: Tools > Linked accounts > Google Analytics 4: not linked. GA4 Admin > Product links > Google Ads: empty.
root_cause: Setup never completed, or property was migrated from UA without re-linking GA4.
fix: Link GA4 ↔ Google Ads in both directions. Import key conversions. Build remarketing audiences in GA4 and make them available to Ads. Enable auto-tagging.
benchmark: Any active account without a GA4–Ads link is failing this check.

pattern_name: Mid-funnel micro-conversions not tracked
symptom: Account tracks only the bottom-of-funnel event (purchase or lead). No tracking for high-intent intermediate actions like 'Book', 'Select room', 'Find listing', 'Add to cart', 'Begin checkout'.
evidence_cue: Conversions list contains only the macro goal; GTM has no triggers for key CTAs on the site.
root_cause: Tracking implemented too narrowly. Smart bidding has no early signals to learn from when conversion volume is low.
fix: Identify 3–5 mid-funnel CTAs, tag them as Secondary conversions, and use them as observation/optimisation aids while the macro goal accumulates volume.
impact_area: Smart bidding learning speed, low-volume campaign optimisation
benchmark: Flag accounts with <30 macro conversions/month and zero secondary conversions configured.

pattern_name: Multiple purchase conversions from different sources counted in parallel
symptom: Account has several 'Purchase' conversions (e.g., from gtag, GA4 import, and Shopify pixel) firing concurrently and inflating reported conversions.
evidence_cue: Multiple Purchase-named conversion actions in the Conversions list; Source column shows different origins; total conversions > actual orders.
root_cause: Tracking layered on without retiring older implementations.
fix: Choose one source of truth for Purchases (usually GA4 import or a single gtag tag). Mark only that one as Primary; demote the rest to Secondary or pause.
benchmark: Investigate when >1 active conversion action represents the same business event.

pattern_name: CRM offline conversion import missing for lead-gen accounts
symptom: Account tracks form submits but no qualified-lead, SQL, or revenue signal is sent back from the CRM. Smart bidding optimises for all leads equally.
evidence_cue: Conversions list has no 'Offline conversion' or 'Enhanced conversions for leads' action; HubSpot/Salesforce connector not configured.
root_cause: CRM not integrated with Google Ads via offline conversions, GCLID/Click ID flow, or Enhanced Conversions for Leads.
fix: Capture GCLID on the form, store it on the CRM record, and pipe qualified-lead/closed-won events back into Ads as offline conversions. Optimise smart bidding against quality, not volume.
benchmark: Required for any lead-gen account spending >$2k/month.

pattern_name: User data / Enhanced Conversions not enabled — remarketing audiences not building
symptom: Remarketing lists show 0 users or are below the 100-user threshold; Enhanced Conversions toggle off.
evidence_cue: Tools > Audience manager: list sizes near zero. Conversions > Settings: Enhanced Conversions disabled.
root_cause: Consent mode misconfigured, user data toggles off, or the global site tag missing on key pages.
fix: Enable Enhanced Conversions, confirm consent mode is configured for the user's region, verify the global tag fires on all pages, and check that remarketing audiences are accumulating users.
benchmark: Flag if any 30-day remarketing list has <100 users in an account with >1,000 monthly sessions.

pattern_name: Advertiser or industry verification incomplete
symptom: Account requires advertiser verification or vertical-specific verification (e.g., financial services in Indonesia) that has not been completed. Ads at risk of disapproval or limited delivery.
evidence_cue: Admin > Account access > Verification status; policy manager notices about verification.
root_cause: Verification process never started or stalled mid-flow (business documents, identity, vertical compliance).
fix: Complete Google's advertiser verification flow. For regulated verticals, submit required licences/registrations. Verify well before scaling spend.
benchmark: Any required verification not completed within 30 days of account creation.

pattern_name: PMax optimised against multiple primary conversion goals
symptom: A single PMax campaign has Purchases, Add-to-cart, Store visits, Phone calls, and Lead messages all flagged as Primary goals.
evidence_cue: Campaign settings > Conversion goals: multiple actions marked Primary.
root_cause: Treating Primary as 'things to count' rather than 'things to optimise toward'. Each Primary action steers bidding.
fix: Keep one Primary per PMax (usually Purchases for e-com). Move the rest to Secondary, or spin up a dedicated PMax for distinct objectives (e.g., a separate Store-Visit PMax).
benchmark: Flag any PMax with >1 Primary conversion goal.

pattern_name: Micro-conversions (Add to Cart, Begin Checkout) kept as Primary after the account has meaningful purchase volume
symptom: Account is past 30+ purchases/month but ATC and Begin Checkout remain Primary, splitting bidding focus from completed purchases.
evidence_cue: Conversions list with ATC/Checkout marked Primary while Purchase volume is healthy.
root_cause: Micro-conversions were added for cold-start learning and never demoted.
fix: Demote ATC and Begin Checkout to Secondary once Purchases hit a stable cadence (≥30/month per campaign). Re-evaluate after 30 days.
benchmark: Apply when Purchase volume ≥30/month and any ATC/Checkout/Pageview event is still Primary.

pattern_name: PMax search themes not configured
symptom: PMax asset groups have audience signals but no search themes — missing an explicit query-level signal to the algorithm.
evidence_cue: Asset group > Search themes panel empty.
root_cause: Search themes are a newer PMax input often overlooked at setup.
fix: Add 5–25 search themes per asset group reflecting the highest-intent queries for that audience or product set. Refresh quarterly.
benchmark: Add when PMax exists with audience signals but zero search themes.

pattern_name: PMax ad assets are weak (low variety in headlines, descriptions, images)
symptom: Asset group is filled with only 1–2 headlines, 1 description, no long headline, few images, and no video.
evidence_cue: Asset group > Assets tab; counts of each asset type vs Google's recommended maxes.
root_cause: Assets uploaded once at launch without subsequent expansion; ad strength reads 'Poor' or 'Average'.
fix: Fill every asset slot up to the recommended max: 5 short headlines, 5 long headlines, 5 descriptions, 15+ images, 5+ videos. Test variants and prune weak performers based on ad strength and asset performance.
benchmark: Add when ad strength is below 'Excellent' or fewer than 50% of asset slots are filled.

pattern_name: Single PMax mixes multiple brands without separation
symptom: One PMax campaign carries all brands; budget gets pulled toward whichever brand the algorithm favours, regardless of business priority.
evidence_cue: Listing groups span multiple brands within a single PMax; brand-level performance variance high.
root_cause: Catalog imported as a single block; no per-brand budget governance.
fix: Split PMax by priority brand or product set so each gets its own budget envelope and bid strategy. Use listing-group exclusions to prevent overlap.
benchmark: Apply when a single PMax contains ≥3 brands with materially different margins or priorities.

pattern_name: PMax used as a 'remarketing-only' campaign — incorrect targeting model
symptom: A PMax campaign is named/structured as remarketing, but PMax uses audience signals as expansion inputs, not strict targeting — so it still serves to new users.
evidence_cue: Campaign named 'PMax | Remarketing'; audience signal set to remarketing list; placements report shows broad new-user reach.
root_cause: Misunderstanding of how PMax audience signals work.
fix: Move remarketing to a dedicated GDN (Display) or Demand Gen campaign with strict audience targeting. Keep PMax for prospecting + lookalike expansion with remarketing as one of several signals.
benchmark: Apply whenever a PMax campaign is intended to serve only existing visitors/customers.

pattern_name: Asset groups within one PMax show large CPA disparity
symptom: One asset group has CPA roughly 2x lower than the others; weaker groups still consume budget.
evidence_cue: Campaign > Asset groups: CPA and conv. value per group.
root_cause: Budget is pooled at campaign level; PMax does not split budget perfectly across asset groups, so winners are starved while losers spend.
fix: Pause underperforming asset groups, or migrate them to a separate PMax with its own (smaller) budget so the high-performer can absorb more budget.
benchmark: Act when an asset group's CPA is ≥1.8x the best-performing group's CPA over 30 days.

pattern_name: Generic 'Smart List' or default audience used as PMax signal
symptom: PMax asset group's audience signal is a vague auto-built list (e.g., 'X-Day Smart List') with no first-party data underneath.
evidence_cue: Asset group > Audience signal: only auto-generated audiences listed.
root_cause: Setup defaulted to Google-suggested audiences without uploading customer lists or layering high-intent events.
fix: Replace with first-party signals: customer list (purchasers, high-LTV), cart abandoners, product viewers. Layer a relevant in-market audience if useful. Refresh quarterly.
benchmark: Replace any PMax signal that doesn't include at least one first-party (customer-list) audience.

pattern_name: AI Max enabled in a Brand campaign matches non-brand queries
symptom: Brand campaign serves on competitor / generic / unrelated queries because AI Max is on by default.
evidence_cue: Search Terms report in the Brand campaign shows non-brand queries; AI Max toggle is enabled in campaign settings.
root_cause: AI Max broadens query matching even on Exact match keywords. In a Brand campaign this defeats the purpose.
fix: Disable AI Max in the Brand campaign. Add aggressive negative keywords for any non-brand themes that have served. Keep AI Max only on prospecting/generic campaigns.
benchmark: Apply to every Brand campaign where AI Max is enabled.

pattern_name: Search campaign uses only Exact match keywords
symptom: Campaigns are 100% Exact match — limiting reach and ignoring relevant query variants.
evidence_cue: Keyword list filtered by match type: all Exact.
root_cause: Conservative setup trying to control intent without leveraging Phrase or Smart-bidding-friendly Broad.
fix: Add Phrase match versions of the best Exact keywords. Test selective Broad match on top performers with Smart bidding. Tight negative list to keep noise out.
benchmark: Apply when 100% of keywords are Exact and the search-impression-share-lost-due-to-rank is >20%.

pattern_name: Search campaign uses only Broad match keywords
symptom: Campaign is built on Broad match only, generating irrelevant queries and wasted spend.
evidence_cue: Keyword list filtered by match type: all Broad. Search Terms report shows high share of off-topic queries.
root_cause: Often a leftover from old 'broad-modified' habits, or set up without active match-type strategy.
fix: Rebuild with a tiered structure: Exact for core converters, Phrase for variants, Broad only paired with Smart bidding and a hardened negative list. Add an account-level negative list.
benchmark: Apply when 100% of keywords are Broad and the Search Terms report shows >25% non-converting query share by spend.

pattern_name: Single ad per ad group — no creative A/B
symptom: Each ad group contains exactly one RSA, blocking any creative testing.
evidence_cue: Ad group > Ads tab shows 1 ad.
root_cause: Setup shortcut. Without 2+ ads, Google can't optimise rotation or learn winning combinations.
fix: Add at least 2 RSAs per ad group with materially different headline and description angles. Let Google rotate; review ad-asset performance every 30 days.
benchmark: Add when any ad group has fewer than 2 active ads.

pattern_name: Ad groups duplicated by match type for the same keyword
symptom: Two ad groups contain the same keyword text — one as Exact, one as Phrase — splitting clicks and complicating reporting.
evidence_cue: Ad group list: pairs of groups with near-identical names differing only by match type.
root_cause: Old SKAG-style structure carried forward. Doesn't help in 2024+ smart-bidding world.
fix: Consolidate match types into a single ad group per keyword theme. Use match-type variety inside the same group; let Smart bidding pick. Migrate winning keywords later to dedicated higher-budget campaigns.
benchmark: Apply whenever ≥50% of ad groups exist as match-type duplicates of another group.

pattern_name: Smart Campaigns used in a performance-focused account
symptom: One or more Smart Campaigns are running, removing keyword, search-term, and creative-level control.
evidence_cue: Campaign type column shows 'Smart' for a performance brand.
root_cause: Created via the simplified flow intended for SMBs without specialist support.
fix: Replace Smart Campaigns with Standard Search (and PMax/Demand Gen where suitable). Migrate budget once the standard campaigns are live and learning.
benchmark: Apply to any account with ≥1 Smart Campaign and monthly spend >$1k.

pattern_name: Semantic core too small — keyword list under-researched
symptom: Search campaigns rely on a handful of keywords (e.g., 11 unique terms) and miss obvious related queries surfacing in the Search Terms report.
evidence_cue: Keyword count per campaign; Search Terms report exposing many high-volume relevant queries not yet on the keyword list.
root_cause: Initial keyword research was shallow. Common when campaigns were set up quickly or with limited time.
fix: Rebuild the semantic core via Keyword Planner, Search Terms report, competitor keyword overlap, and on-site search data. Group by intent and theme before launching.
benchmark: Investigate if a campaign has <20 unique keywords and ≥10 high-volume relevant queries appear in Search Terms but not as keywords.

pattern_name: Ad strength below 'Excellent' caps Quality Score
symptom: RSA ad strength reads 'Good' or 'Average' — Quality Score and ad position suffer; CPC is higher than necessary.
evidence_cue: Ad group > Ads tab: Ad strength column.
root_cause: Insufficient or under-varied headlines, descriptions, and assets; weak keyword presence in headlines.
fix: Add headlines and descriptions until count and uniqueness hit 'Excellent'. Include 1–2 keywords from the ad group in pinned headlines. Add or update assets.
benchmark: Improve any ad with ad strength below 'Excellent' in a high-spend ad group.

pattern_name: Fewer than 4 sitelinks or no structured snippets configured
symptom: Account or campaign has <4 sitelinks and/or zero structured snippets, missing SERP real estate.
evidence_cue: Assets > Sitelinks and Structured snippets, by campaign.
root_cause: Setup didn't complete the asset library.
fix: Add 6+ sitelinks and 2+ structured snippets per campaign. Use product/service categories. Test multiple variants and prune low CTR.
benchmark: Apply when sitelinks <4 or structured snippets = 0 per campaign.

pattern_name: Bids too low — important keywords consistently below top-3 position
symptom: Average CPC is high relative to position; key keywords have low Ad Rank; impression share lost to rank is large.
evidence_cue: Top vs. Other Impression Share; Search top IS lost (rank).
root_cause: Manual CPC bids set conservatively or Target ROAS/Target CPA too tight for the auction landscape, combined with weak ad strength and Quality Score.
fix: Raise bids on the keywords that matter; improve ad strength in parallel to lift Quality Score so the same money buys higher positions; reconsider Target CPA/ROAS values.
benchmark: Investigate when Search top IS lost (rank) >25% on revenue-driving keywords.

pattern_name: Search and Display traffic combined in a single campaign
symptom: A 'Search' campaign has 'Display Network' enabled — mixing high-intent and low-intent traffic in one budget and one bid strategy.
evidence_cue: Campaign settings > Networks: Display checked alongside Search.
root_cause: Default 'Include Google Display Network' option left on at creation.
fix: Disable Display in Search campaigns. Spin up a separate GDN campaign for Display with its own creatives, audiences, and budget.
benchmark: Apply to any Search campaign where Display Network is enabled.

pattern_name: Dynamic Keyword Insertion (DKI) opportunity for ad relevance
symptom: Generic RSAs across many keywords with no DKI; ad strength stuck below 'Excellent'.
evidence_cue: Ad copy doesn't use DKI snippets or location insertion. Many keyword themes in one ad group.
root_cause: Static copy can't keep up with keyword variety; missing a low-effort relevance lift.
fix: Add DKI in one headline per ad group (with a sensible default). Test DKI vs static for 30 days; measure CTR and Quality Score impact.
benchmark: Apply when an ad group has >5 distinct keyword themes and DKI is unused.

pattern_name: Multiple countries combined into a single campaign
symptom: One campaign targets several countries with shared budget and bidding — performance signals from different markets blend.
evidence_cue: Campaign > Locations: multiple country-level rows.
root_cause: Setup shortcut. User behaviour, competition, conversion rates, and seasonality differ materially by country.
fix: Split into one campaign per priority country (or per region/cluster). Allocate budgets per market, set localised assets and (where possible) currencies.
benchmark: Apply when a single campaign targets ≥3 countries with materially different CPMs or conversion rates.

pattern_name: Top markets need their own dedicated campaigns
symptom: Top-performing country/region is bundled inside a global or multi-country campaign and isn't getting its own budget/creative focus.
evidence_cue: Locations report: top country drives >30% of conversions but shares budget with low performers.
root_cause: No mechanism to push budget into the proven market.
fix: Lift top markets into their own Search and PMax campaigns with dedicated budgets, localized assets, and tight goals. Keep test/long-tail markets in a separate exploratory campaign.
impact_area: Spend allocation, growth in proven markets
benchmark: Apply when any single country contributes ≥30% of conversions but doesn't have a dedicated campaign.

pattern_name: Ads serving outside the intended country
symptom: Locations report shows impressions and clicks from countries the business doesn't serve.
evidence_cue: Campaign > Locations report; 'Presence or interest' setting; user location vs. interest report.
root_cause: Default 'Presence or interest' setting bids on people merely interested in the target country, not just located there.
fix: Switch to 'Presence (people in or regularly in your targeted locations)' on campaigns where physical presence matters. Add specific country exclusions if needed.
benchmark: Apply whenever spend outside intended country/countries is >5% of monthly spend.

pattern_name: Over-granular location targeting on a small budget
symptom: Campaign targets 2,000+ city/zip locations on a budget too small to fill impressions in any of them.
evidence_cue: Locations panel: thousands of granular rows; daily spend low; impression volume thin per location.
root_cause: Manual location lists exported and bulk-added without considering budget feasibility.
fix: Collapse to state/region targeting (or country-with-bid-modifiers). Let Smart bidding find the converting micro-geos. Revisit granular targeting only after volume justifies it.
benchmark: Apply when location count ≥1,000 and daily budget <$150.

pattern_name: Conversions concentrated in specific age bands — no age bid adjustments
symptom: Demographics report shows conversions concentrated in 18–44 (or any specific band), but no age bid adjustments applied.
evidence_cue: Audiences > Demographics > Age: conversion rate variance with no bid adjustment column populated.
root_cause: Default settings bid equally across all age bands.
fix: Apply positive bid adjustments to the converting bands; negative or full exclusion on non-converting bands once enough data has accumulated.
benchmark: Apply when one age band converts at ≥1.5x the account average across 90 days with ≥30 conversions in that band.

pattern_name: Language targeting too narrow for the market
symptom: Campaigns target only one language (e.g., Ukrainian) in a market where users browse in multiple languages.
evidence_cue: Campaign > Languages: single language selected.
root_cause: Conservative default. Reach is restricted to users whose browser/device language matches.
fix: Add additional languages relevant to the target audience (e.g., English, Russian, plus 'All languages' as a test). Re-evaluate reach and conversion volume after 30 days.
benchmark: Apply when a campaign targets one language in a multi-language market and impression volume is below target.

pattern_name: Whole account budget concentrated in a single campaign at launch
symptom: 100% of budget is allocated to one campaign at account launch, with no historical data and no diversification by funnel/geo/strategy.
evidence_cue: Campaign list: 1 active campaign; spend chart shows full budget on one line.
root_cause: Single-strategy bet without baseline data. High risk of wasting budget if the chosen setup is wrong.
fix: Diversify into 2–4 test campaigns covering different bidding strategies, audience segments, or funnel stages. Run for a learning period before consolidating around winners.
benchmark: Apply when account has ≤1 campaign in the first 30 days and spend >$2k.

pattern_name: Budget too low for Smart bidding to learn in the niche
symptom: Campaigns are starving — daily budget below average CPA × ~10 conversions; Smart bidding stays in 'Learning' indefinitely.
evidence_cue: Status column shows 'Limited by budget' or 'Learning'; conversion volume <20/30 days.
root_cause: Budget set without reference to auction CPC and required conversion volume for the chosen strategy.
fix: Raise daily budget so the campaign can hit at least 20 conversions over 30 days under the chosen bid strategy. If budget can't grow, drop to Manual CPC or Maximize Clicks while signals accumulate.
benchmark: Increase budget when 'Learning' status persists >2 weeks and conversion volume <20/30 days.

pattern_name: Brand Search using Target ROAS / Max Conv Value instead of Manual CPC
symptom: Brand campaign uses a value-based smart strategy; Google holds back impressions on cheap, high-intent brand clicks chasing arbitrary ROAS targets.
evidence_cue: Brand campaign bid strategy = Target ROAS or Maximize Conversion Value; impression share lost to budget/rank in single digits but lost to other reasons.
root_cause: Brand traffic already converts heavily; smart bidding over-throttles brand impressions, leaving easy revenue on the table.
fix: Switch Brand Search to Manual CPC (or Maximize Clicks with low CPC cap). Capture every available brand click; control cost via bid cap.
benchmark: Apply to any Brand campaign on a value-bid strategy with absolute Top Impression Share <90%.

pattern_name: Maximize Conversions strategy on campaigns with <20 conversions/30 days
symptom: Smart bidding active on a campaign that doesn't have the data volume to power it; results swing wildly.
evidence_cue: Campaign settings show 'Maximize Conversions'; conversion count <20 in last 30 days.
root_cause: Smart bidding under-fed; the algorithm cannot stabilise on so few signals.
fix: Drop to Manual CPC or Maximize Clicks while volume grows. Re-evaluate at 30+ conversions per 30 days before re-enabling Smart bidding.
benchmark: Switch off when a Max Conv / Max Conv Value strategy is running on <20 conv/30 days.

pattern_name: Smart bidding (Max Conversions / tROAS / tCPA) at launch without historical data
symptom: New campaign launches directly on a Smart bidding strategy with no conversion history in the account.
evidence_cue: Campaign created <30 days; bid strategy is automated; conversion column near zero.
root_cause: Smart bidding needs historical conversion data to perform. Without it, the algorithm wastes the first month exploring.
fix: Launch new campaigns on Manual CPC or Maximize Clicks for 2–4 weeks to accumulate baseline data, then migrate to Smart bidding once 20–30 conversions exist.
benchmark: Apply to any campaign launching on automated bidding with <50 historical conversions in the account.

pattern_name: tCPA target too tight, throttling impression delivery
symptom: Campaign spends well below its daily budget (e.g., $162 of $210/day) because the tCPA target is unrealistic for the auction.
evidence_cue: Spend vs Budget gap; bid strategy shows 'Target CPA'; Search top IS lost (rank/budget) low while impression count low.
root_cause: Target CPA is below what the auction allows; Google can't find traffic that meets the threshold.
fix: Loosen the tCPA target (+15–25%) for the next 14 days, monitor CPA drift, and tighten again once volume stabilises. Alternatively, expand keywords/audience so cheaper conversions are reachable.
benchmark: Apply when actual spend is <60% of daily budget for 14+ days on a tCPA strategy.

pattern_name: Account runs only Search — no PMax, DSA, Demand Gen, or remarketing
symptom: Campaign type breakdown shows 100% Search. No PMax, DSA, Demand Gen, GDN remarketing, or YouTube activity.
evidence_cue: Campaigns view filtered by Type; only Search appears.
root_cause: Conservative or legacy account; missed efficiency that PMax/DSA/remarketing routinely deliver.
fix: Layer in PMax for catalog-driven categories or DSA for content-heavy sites. Add a remarketing campaign (GDN or Demand Gen). Test Demand Gen for upper-funnel awareness.
benchmark: Apply to any account with monthly spend >$5k where Search is the only active campaign type.

pattern_name: Ad copy is stale — no seasonal refresh routine
symptom: Same ads have been running unchanged for many months; messaging doesn't reflect current promos, seasons, or user concerns.
evidence_cue: Ads tab: last edited dates >6 months ago across most ad groups.
root_cause: No recurring creative cadence; testing isn't built into the optimisation routine.
fix: Set a 30/60/90-day creative refresh cycle. Update headlines/descriptions seasonally. Test 2 new RSA variants per ad group per quarter. Align messaging to demand drivers (convenience, price, urgency, safety).
benchmark: Refresh when ads haven't been materially edited in >120 days.

pattern_name: Disapproved ad extension or logo asset
symptom: Sitelink, callout, image, or logo asset shows 'Disapproved' status — assets aren't serving.
evidence_cue: Assets tab > Status column.
root_cause: Policy violations (logo format/size, prohibited content, trademark, etc.) or stale assets predating policy changes.
fix: Fix or replace the disapproved asset; submit re-review where appropriate; ensure logo matches Google's format/size requirements.
benchmark: Resolve any disapproved asset open for >7 days.

pattern_name: Final URL doesn't follow system recommendations / landing-page quality flagged
symptom: Recommendations panel flags landing-page issues (broken URL, slow load, mobile usability, mismatch with ad).
evidence_cue: Recommendations tab; ad-status warnings; PageSpeed Insights score for the final URL.
root_cause: Final URL not maintained as the campaign and product mix evolve; mobile performance regressions.
fix: Update final URLs to the most relevant high-performing landing page. Fix PageSpeed issues. Use ad-group-level final URLs for tighter ad-to-page alignment.
benchmark: Resolve any open landing-page recommendation that affects ≥10% of spend.

pattern_name: Ad headlines start with a lowercase letter
symptom: Headlines in the ads tab begin with lowercase words ('best deals…') reducing perceived professionalism and CTR.
evidence_cue: Ads tab: visual scan of headlines.
root_cause: Copy mistakes carried into live ads.
fix: Edit headlines to use sentence case or title case. Use a copy-review checklist before publishing.
benchmark: Fix any RSA with a headline starting with lowercase.

pattern_name: Image, price, and callout extensions missing
symptom: Account has no image, price, or callout extensions configured — leaving SERP real estate and CTR uplift on the table.
evidence_cue: Assets > by type: missing rows for Image, Price, Callout extensions.
root_cause: Setup didn't include these newer/optional extensions.
fix: Add image extensions (1.91:1 and 1:1), price extensions for top services/products, and 4+ callouts. Test variants over 30 days.
benchmark: Add when any of these extension types is absent at the campaign level.

pattern_name: App placement exclusion list incomplete
symptom: Display/PMax serving in mobile apps; only a partial list of app exclusions is applied (e.g., 92 of 140).
evidence_cue: Content > Exclusions > Apps; placement performance report shows app-category placements.
root_cause: Exclusion list built once and not maintained; new apps keep entering inventory.
fix: Apply the full account-level app/category exclusion list (often 'all apps' for retail/lead-gen). Maintain exclusions monthly.
benchmark: Apply when app placements >2% of spend in any Display/PMax campaign.

pattern_name: Own domain not excluded as referral source in GA4/UA
symptom: GA4 reports show the brand's own domain as a referral, breaking session/source integrity and undercrediting paid channels.
evidence_cue: GA4 Admin > Data streams > Configure tag settings > Unwanted referrals.
root_cause: Default config doesn't always include all subdomains/checkout domains.
fix: Add the brand's domain and any cart/checkout subdomains to Unwanted Referrals in GA4. Re-validate session source/medium in test purchases.
benchmark: Fix whenever the brand's own domain appears in the GA4 referral report.

pattern_name: Call tracking not enabled for a phone-driven business
symptom: Account drives leads via phone (clinic, service, real estate, etc.) but has no Google call extension conversion or third-party call tracking.
evidence_cue: Conversions list: no 'Calls from ads' or 'Website calls' conversion; call extensions exist but not as conversion sources.
root_cause: Call extensions added without enabling them as conversions; or no dynamic phone replacement on the site.
fix: Turn on Google call conversions for call extensions; add a website call conversion with a minimum length; for richer attribution, use a third-party call-tracking provider that ports calls back as offline conversions.
benchmark: Required for any lead-gen account where phone is a primary contact channel.

pattern_name: Lead Form Asset missing on a Lead-goal campaign
symptom: Campaign is set up with the 'Leads' objective but no Lead Form Asset is attached; conversions rely entirely on the website form.
evidence_cue: Campaign > Assets > Lead form: empty.
root_cause: Setup oversight; the asset is opt-in.
fix: Add a Lead Form Asset with targeted qualifying questions; set the form-submit as a Primary conversion or a Secondary one alongside the website form; verify a CRM connection so leads flow in.
benchmark: Apply to any Leads-goal campaign without a Lead Form Asset.

pattern_name: Product feed not segmented by gender / collection — wrong items served
symptom: Men's items appear in women's-targeted campaigns (or vice versa); ROAS suffers and brand consistency breaks.
evidence_cue: Shopping/PMax placements show off-target products; Merchant Center feed lacks proper 'gender' attribute or custom_label segmentation.
root_cause: Feed isn't segmented by gender/collection; campaigns target broad listing groups.
fix: Add gender/custom_label attributes in the feed; create gender- or collection-specific campaigns/asset groups; exclude opposite-gender SKUs from each.
benchmark: Apply when ≥10% of Shopping/PMax impressions show off-gender products.

pattern_name: Merchant Center has disapproved products (missing price, image, or other required attributes)
symptom: A portion of active SKUs in Merchant Center are disapproved or pending, missing price, image, GTIN, or shipping data.
evidence_cue: Merchant Center > Products > Diagnostics; disapprovals by issue type.
root_cause: Feed quality drift; required attributes missing; image/landing-page issues.
fix: Fix the highest-impact disapprovals first (missing price, missing image, mismatched URL). Add a weekly diagnostics review. Use supplemental feeds if upstream catalog can't be corrected immediately.
benchmark: Resolve any account where >5% of SKUs are disapproved.

pattern_name: PMax audience signal excludes 'Unknown' gender or age
symptom: Asset-group signals exclude Unknown gender/age, shrinking the eligible signal pool.
evidence_cue: Audience signal > Demographics: Unknown unchecked.
root_cause: Tight demographic targeting habits carried over from Search/Display, where signals act as filters. In PMax they act as learning inputs.
fix: Re-include Unknown gender/age unless performance data clearly justifies exclusion. PMax signals are inputs, not strict filters.
benchmark: Apply any time Unknown gender/age is unchecked without a clear performance reason.

pattern_name: Underperforming assets (sitelinks/snippets/headlines) not paused
symptom: Asset performance report flags 'Low' performers, but they continue to serve and drag down ad combinations.
evidence_cue: Assets tab > Performance column: 'Low' or 'Best/Good/Low' breakdown showing weak items.
root_cause: No recurring asset review.
fix: Monthly: pause/replace 'Low' performers; promote 'Best' assets to additional ad groups. Maintain ad strength at 'Excellent'.
benchmark: Prune any asset rated 'Low' for >30 days with sufficient impressions.

pattern_name: Paused historical campaigns outperformed current active campaigns
symptom: Change history reveals previously paused campaigns (PMax/Search) had lower CPA / higher ROAS than today's active mix.
evidence_cue: Change history; previously-active campaigns with stronger performance than current ones.
root_cause: Strategy pivots happened without comparing outgoing vs incoming campaign performance.
fix: Reactivate the top historical campaigns at modest budgets, run alongside current ones for 30 days, scale winners. Migrate learnings (audience signals, creatives) from the historical setup.
benchmark: Revive any paused campaign with ≥60 days of history at CPA ≤80% of current average.

pattern_name: Suspect conversion: conversions outnumber clicks on a query/asset
symptom: A search term shows 4 clicks but 11 conversions on a 'consult apply' event — clear sign the conversion is firing multiple times per session or counting a non-conversion interaction.
evidence_cue: Search Terms or Asset performance: conversions > clicks at the row level.
root_cause: Conversion is configured as 'Every' instead of 'One', counting button clicks or pageviews, or duplicated by stacked tags.
fix: Change count setting to 'One'. Audit the underlying trigger — if it tracks a button click rather than a checkout completion, replace it. Remove duplicate tags.
benchmark: Investigate any conversion action where conversions > clicks for individual rows.
